sentences.text: Alzheimer's Disease Tests Number: 0349 (Replaces CPB 436) Applicable CPT / HCPCS / ICD-10 Codes Scope of Policy This Clinical Policy Bulletin addresses Alzheimer's disease tests.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: I. Medical Necessity Aetna considers the following tests medically necessary for the diagnosis or management of Alzheimer's disease (AD) when criteria are met: A. Lumbar puncture to confirm at least one of the following detected in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) as determined by the lab assay for assessment of mild cognitive impairment (MCI) when AD is suspected: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Presence of elevated phosphorylated tau (P-tau) protein and/or elevated total tau (T-tau) protein, and reduced beta amyloid-42 (AB42); or 2. Low AB42/AB40 ratio; or 3. Elevated P-tau/AB42 ratio; or 4.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Elevated T-tau/AB42 ratio; B. Brain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) for any the following indications in persons on Aduhelm, Leqembi, or Kisunla therapy: see CPB 0996 - Aducanumab-avwa (Aduhelm), CPB 1026 - Lecanemab-irmb (Leqembi), or CPB 1066 - Donanemab-azbt (Kisunla). 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Within one year prior to initiating aducanumab-avwa (Aduhelm), lecanemab-irmb (Leqembi), or donanemab-azbt (Kisunla) therapy to indicate AD; or 2.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: To evaluate for evidence of amyloid-related imaging abnormalities (ARIA) prior to the 5th, 7th, 9th, and 12th aducanumab (Aduhelm) dose, prior to 5th, 7th, and 14th lecanemab-irmb (Leqembi) dose, or prior to 2nd dose, 3rd dose, 4th dose, and 7th donanemab-azbt (Kisunla) dose; C. For amyloid PET scan indications, see CPB 0071 - Positron Emission Tomography (PET); D. Genetic mutation testing for amyloid precursor protein (APP), presenilin-1 (PSEN1), or presenilin-2 (PSEN2) for members with MCI or mild AD dementia who are less than 50 years of age and are being considered for aducanumab (Aduhelm) or lecanemab-irmb (Leqembi) therapy.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: For Aduhelm, member must be enrolled in a clinical trial.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: See CPB 0996 - Aducanumab-avwa (Aduhelm) or CPB 1026 - Lecanemab-irmb (Leqembi).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: E. Apolipoprotein E 4 (ApoE4) testing for members with MCI or mild AD dementia being considered for aducanumab (Aduhelm), lecanemab-irmb (Leqembi), or donanemab-azbt (Kisunla) therapy.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: For Aduhelm, member must be enrolled in a clinical trial.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: See CPB 0996 - Aducanumab-avwa (Aduhelm), CPB 1026 - Lecanemab-irmb, or CPB 1066 - Donanemab-azbt (Kisunla). II.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven The following tests/measurements are considered experimental, investigational, or unproven for the diagnosis and/or assessment of members with AD and related dementias (not an all-inclusive list): Apolipoprotein E (ApoE) for all other indications not listed in Section I Artificial intelligence and machine learning applications ATP-binding cassette transporter (ABCA7) Automated computerized cognitive assessment aids Bcl-2 rs956572 polymorphism testing Beta-site amyloid precursor protein cleaving enzyme 1 (BACE1) Blood neurodegenerative analytes (e.g., levels of cholesterol, ferritin and iron) as biomarkers in AD progression Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) alpha-synuclein for prediction of early disease progression in AD) CSF chitinase enzyme activity Circadian rhythm analysis CSF golgin A4 CSF microRNAs (e.g., hsa-miR-27a-3p) CSF prion protein concentration CSF synaptic biomarkers (e.g., GAP-43, neurogranin, SNAP-25 total, SNAP-25aa40, synaptotagmin-1) CSF soluble amyloid precursor proteins (sAPP) level/CSF β-secretase activity CSF stathmin protein level CSF visinin-like protein-1 (VILIP-1) level Cognitive event-related potentials (cognitive evoked potentials) DNA methylation profiling (brain tissue or peripheral blood) Electronystagmography (in the absence of signs of vertigo or balance disorder) Evaluation of Helicobacter pylori infection for prediction of risk of AD Genetic variation of mitochondrial DNA Gustatory function testing Homocysteine (serum level) INNO-BIA AlzBio3 immunoassay kit (a multiplex immunoassay that allows simultaneous quantification of amyloid-beta, p-tau, and t-tau) Insulin degrading enzyme polymorphisms Long-term measurement of cortisol LucentAD Complete Macular thickness Microtubule-associated protein tau (MAPT) MindX Blood Test - Memory/Alzheimer's Monokine-induced gamma interferon (MIG) Morphometric imaging, protein kinase C-epsilon (PKCe), and quantitative imaging of phosphorylated Erk1 and Erk2 (e.g., Discern test; NeuroDiagnostics, LLC) N-terminal pro-brain natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP) Olfactory screening tests Particulate matter with an aerodynamic diameter of less than or equal to 2.5 μm (PM2.5) Plasma Beta amyloid 40 (Aβ40) protein Plasma Beta amyloid 42 (Aβ42) protein Plasma clusterin level Plasma glial fibrillary acidic protein (as a biomarker for progression of AD) Plasma lipoproteome Plasma prion protein concentration Plasma tau – including phosphorylated tau (P-tau) (p-tau231, p-tau217 [e.g., ALZpath pTau217]] and/or total tau (T-tau) protein Plasma YKL-30 Pituitary adenylate cyclase-activating polypeptide (PACAP) PrecivityAD2 test Precivity-ApoE Red blood cell omega-3 fatty acid level Remote monitoring for early detection of AD Resting state eye-closed cortical electroencephalography Retinal microvascular changes (as biomarkers for early diagnosis of AD and monitoring of disease progression) Serum ceramides Serum insulin-like growth factor-1 (IGF-1; also known as somatomedin C) Serum microRNAs Serum neurofilament light concentration Serum triglycerides Syn-One test (cutaneous alpha-synuclein testing) for the diagnosis and assessment of AD and related dementias Toxoplasma gondii Transforming growth factor-beta1 (TGF-β1) TREM2 (triggering receptor expressed on myeloid cells 2) Tympanometry (in the absence of hearing loss) Urinary AD7c-NTP (neuronal thread protein/neural thread protein) Use of astrocyte biomarkers in the management of AD Videopupillography and tropicamide drop test. III.
sentences.label: CLAIM

epilepsy and other diseases of the nervous system [family history of Alzheimer's disease] Alzheimer disease (AD) is the most common form of dementia.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: It is a neurologic condition characterized by loss of mental ability severe enough to interfere with normal activities of daily living, lasting at least six months and not present from birth.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: AD usually occurs in adulthood and is marked by a decline in cognitive functions such as remembering, reasoning and planning.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The diagnosis of AD is a clinical diagnosis, focusing on the exclusion of other causes of senile dementia.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Both the tau and BA-42 molecules are components of the neurofibrillary tangles associated with AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Levels of these molecules found in the cerebro-spinal fluid (CSF) have been investigated as a diagnostic test for AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Additionally, there is an association between the apolipoprotein E (apoE) epsilon 4 genotype and AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The apoE genotype consists of one of several different combinations of the 3 different alleles, which are labeled 2, 3 or 4.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: It has been shown that the presence of one allele (apoE4) is over-represented in patients with late onset AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: There is inadequate data regarding the positive and negative predictive values of CSF levels of tau and BA- 42 in the diagnosis of AD in patients with clinical symptoms consistent with possible AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: While the presence of an apoE4 allele may be associated with an increased risk of AD, the associated positive and negative predictive values are inadequate to validate apoE genotyping as a diagnostic test for AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Additionally, there is no information regarding how such testing would influence the management of the patient.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A high positive predictive value may not be clinically useful since the diagnosis can be made clinically.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The Agency for Health Care Policy and Research Clinical Practice Guideline addressed the diagnosis and assessment of AD and related dementias.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The guideline stated that "it is not yet possible to depend on apoE genotyping for definitive guidance about diagnosis or treatment of Alzheimer's disease" (Costa et al, 1996).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Furthermore, the algorithm presented in the Guideline for the recognition and initial assessment of AD did not incorporate measurement of CSF levels of the peptides tau and BA-42.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The Alzheimer's Disease Guidelines Panel concluded that the role of these markers in the diagnosis and management of patients with AD are questions for further research.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Genetic tests are laboratory studies of human deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA), chromosomes, genes or gene products to diagnose the presence of a variation associated with a high risk of having or transmitting a specific genetic disorder.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Genetic screening for persons who may be at high risk for AD, such as those with the apolipoprotein E 4 gene locus on chromosome 19, is a highly debated issue, particularly because currently no demonstrated intervention can prevent or delay the dementing process, and no evidence exists about predicting age of onset for person with this genotype.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Identifiable genetic mutations are rare causes of AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Persons with early onset of AD (before age 65) may show an autosomal dominant pattern of inheritance.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Nearly all of the autosomal dominant familial forms of AD are related to mutations in one of three different genes: mutations of the amyloid precursor protein (APP) gene on chromosome 21 and genes encoding presenilin 1 (PS1) on chromosome 14 and presenilin 2 (PS2) on chromosome 1.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: However, only 2 to 10 % of all persons with AD have early onset disease, and genetic mutations have been identified in 30 to 50 % of these patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In addition, although detection of genetic mutations may have prognostic significance, there is no evidence that genetic testing for AD would alter the management of patients such that clinical outcomes are improved.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The PS-1 DNA Sequencing Test, replacing the Symptomatic PS-1 Analysis and Interpretation Test (Athena Diagnostics), is used for evaluating patients with progressive demential with onset before age 65 with a positive family history of early-onset AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: It detects sequence variations in the presenilin 1 (PS-1) gene by means of polymerase chain reaction and DNA sequencing.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: However, there is insufficient evidence to support its clinical value at this time.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Efforts to develop biologic markers for the presence of AD, such as tests that could be performed on samples of blood or CSF, are important research topics for confirming a suspected diagnosis of AD.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: According to the Canadian Consensus Conference on Dementia (1999) and the American Academy of Neurology's practice parameter on Diagnosis of Dementia (2001), the usefulness of tests for tau, BA-42, and apoE for the diagnosis of AD has not been established.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The AD-associated neuronal thread protein (AD7c-NTP) gene encodes an approximately 41 kD membrane- spanning phosphoprotein that causes apoptosis and neuritic sprouting in transfected neuronal cells.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The AD7c-NTP gene is over-expressed in AD beginning early in the course of disease.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The levels of neuronal thread protein in post-mortem brain tissue correlate with the levels measured in paired ventricular fluid samples, suggesting that the protein is secreted or released by dying cells into CSF.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Recent studies have suggested that urine test for AD7c-NTP could be used to assess the risk of developing AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: de la Monte and Wands (2002) reported that elevated levels of AD7c-NTP can be detected in both CSF and urine of patients with early or moderately severe AD, and the CSF and urinary levels of AD7c-NTP correlate with the severity of dementia.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors reported that the newest configuration of the AD7c-NTP assay, termed "7c Gold", has greater than 90 % sensitivity and specificity for detecting early AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Munzar et al (2002) reported that the competitive enzyme-linked immuno-sorbent assay (ELISA)-format AD7C-NTP test in urine is an accurate method for determining AD7c-NTP levels in AD and could be used as a biochemical marker for AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Additional studies are needed to validate these preliminary results and to demonstrate the impact of AD7c- NTP screening on clinical outcomes.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: In a 2001 American Academy of Neurology's practice parameter for the diagnosis of dementia (Knopman et al, 2001) stated that "no laboratory tests have yet emerged that are appropriate for routine use in the clinical evaluation of patients with suspected AD".
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The practice parameter concluded that further research is needed to improve clinical definitions of dementia and its subtypes, as well as to determine the utility of various instruments of neuroimaging, biomarkers, and genetic testing in increasing diagnostic accuracy.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The tropicamide drop test has been proposed as a rapid, non-invasive method for the early diagnosis of AD based on the observation that patients with AD exhibit greater pupillary dilation following administration of a diluted solution (0.01 %) of the cholinergic antagonist, tropicamide.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: However, subsequent studies reported that pupillary response to tropicamide does not differentiate between AD patients and healthy subjects.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The tropicamide drop test is associated with high individual variability in the pupillary response to topically applied drugs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Furthermore, the reliability (test-retest) of the tropicamide drop test is questionable.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Mild cognitive impairment is a transition period between physiological aging and dementia.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Each year more than 12 % of individuals with mild cognitive impairment develop AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In a controlled study, Eibenstein and colleagues (2005) assessed the presence of an olfactory deficit in patients with amnesic mild cognitive impairment (aMCI).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A total of 29 subjects diagnosed with aMCI and a homogeneous control group of 29 subjects were enrolled in the study.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Olfactory function was assessed by the Sniffin' Sticks Screening Test (SSST) and the Mini Mental State Examination, the Clinical Dementia Rating, the Geriatric Depression Scale and the Mental Deterioration Battery were used to evaluate the neurocognitive status.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Individuals with aMCI showed a significant impairment of their olfactory identification compared to controls (SSST score: 8.3 +/- 2.1 versus 10.8 +/- 0.9; p < 0.001).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These results suggested that olfactory tests should be part of the diagnostic armamentarium of pre-clinical dementia.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: They noted that a long-term follow-up might confirm the olfactory identification function as an early and reliable marker in the diagnosis of pre-clinical dementia.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Hampel and Shen (2009) noted that AD is characterized by the progressive formation of insoluble amyloid plaques and vascular deposits consisting of the amyloid beta-peptide (Abeta) in the brain.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Pathological mechanisms are already active early in the pre-symptomatic stage of AD.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Beta-site amyloid precursor protein cleaving enzyme 1 (BACE1), also known as beta-secretase, is one of the 2 key enzymes in APP processing; the other being gamma-secretase.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The Abeta peptide results from cleavage of APP initially by BACE1 to produce the C99 fragment and releases soluble APPbeta (sAPPbeta); C99 is then further cleaved by gamma-secretase leading to the Abeta peptide.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Increased BACE1 activity and elevated levels of insoluble Abeta peptide have been shown in brain tissue of patients with sporadic AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Since the CSF is in direct contact with the extra-cellular space of the central nervous system, biochemical changes in the brain can potentially be reflected in CSF.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Thus, CSF-based detection of BACE1 levels and activity might be valuable in aiding early detection and prediction, particularly in pre-clinical or even pre-symptomatic subjects who are at risk of AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Recently, these researchers were among the first groups to quantitatively analyze the enzymatic activities and protein levels of BACE1 in the CSF.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Preliminary research using recently developed BACE1 ELISAs, BACE1 enzymatic activity, sAPPbeta and total Abeta1-x ELISAs were used by examining these hypothesis driven functional candidate markers in subjects with clinically diagnosed AD and amnestic mild cognitive impairment (MCI).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Two sandwich ELISAs were used and BACE1 enzymatic activities were seen by synthetic fluorescence substrate and total Abeta levels by sandwich-ELISA.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Moreover, elevated CSF levels of BACE1 protein were associated with an increased risk ratio in MCI.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Interestingly, amnestic MCI subjects showed increased levels of BACE1 activity compared to healthy controls (HC) and AD patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: For total Abeta and tau, increased CSF levels were associated with a higher risk of MCI compared to HC as well.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: BACE1 activity was significantly correlated with BACE1 protein concentration and total Abeta levels, with Abeta being itself correlated with the BACE1 protein level.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that currently, independent studies are ongoing to validate BACE1 and functionally associated proteins as candidate biomarkers for early detection, prediction, progression as well as for biological activity in AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Schmand and colleagues (2010) noted that abnormal levels of biomarkers in CSF and atrophy of medial temporal lobe (MTL) structures on magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) are being used increasingly to diagnose early AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These investigators evaluated the claim that these biomarkers can detect pre-clinical AD before behavioral (i.e., memory) symptoms arise.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: They included all relevant longitudinal studies of CSF and MRI biomarkers published between January 2003 and November 2008.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Subjects were not demented at baseline but some declined to MCI or to AD during follow-up.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Measures of tau and beta-amyloid in CSF, MTL atrophy on MRI, and performance on delayed memory tasks were extracted from the papers or obtained from the investigators.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: A total of 21 MRI studies and 14 CSF studies were retrieved.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The effect sizes of total tau, phosphorylated tau and amyloid beta 42 ranged from 0.91 to 1.11.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The effect size of MTL atrophy was 0.75.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Memory performance had an effect size of 1.06.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Atrophy of MTL and memory impairment tended to increase when assessed closer to the moment of diagnosis, whereas effect sizes of CSF biomarkers tended to increase when assessed longer before the diagnosis.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that memory impairment is a more accurate predictor of early AD than atrophy of MTL on MRI, whereas CSF abnormalities and memory impairment are about equally predictive.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Consequently, the CSF and MRI biomarkers are not very sensitive to pre-clinical AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Cerebro-spinal fluid markers remain promising, but studies with long follow-up periods in elderly subjects who are normal at baseline are needed to evaluate this promise.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In a cross-sectional study, Carlesimo et al (2010) examined the relationship between age-related memory decline and MRI hippocampal anatomical changes in a cohort of healthy individuals.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A total of 76 healthy individuals (44 males and 32 females), ranging in age from 20 to 80 years, were recruited.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: These individuals were submitted to a 3-T MRI protocol with a whole-brain T1-weighted and diffusion-weighted scanning and a neuropsychological assessment.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: For each subject, these researchers calculated the volumes of the total brain (gray + white matter) and hippocampi.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The segmented hippocampi defined the binary masks where mean values of mean diffusivity (MD) and fractional anisotropy (FA) were calculated.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Neuropsychological evaluation included tests of verbal memory (15-min delayed recall of a 15-word list) and visuospatial memory (20-min delayed reproduction of Rey complex figure).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Hippocampal MD, but not hippocampal FA, hippocampal volume, or total brain volume, predicted performance of individuals beyond their 50s on tests of verbal as well as visuo-spatial memory.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The author concluded that high mean diffusivity values in the hippocampal formation of healthy elderly individuals predict memory decline, as reflected by performance on tests of declarative verbal and visual-spatial memory.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In the present study, none of the subjects fulfilled the clinical or neuropsychological criteria for MCI.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Nevertheless, in a number of subjects beyond their 50s, high MD values in the hippocampal formation were accompanied by performance scores that were not truly pathological, but fell in the lower portion of the normal range on tests of declarative memory.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The relevant question is if these individuals represent a very early stage in the progression of AD, anterior to the development of an aMCI, or whether, instead, they represent the lower portion of the normal distribution formed by aged individuals who will not develop dementia.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors stated that longitudinal studies that follow the outcome of these individuals are needed to discriminate between these 2 alternative hypotheses.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: In an editorial that accompanied the afore-mentioned article, Schuff (2010) noted that these findings by Carlesimo et al raise several important issues such as I. they imply that memory deficits in healthy individuals have a biologic underpinning without apparent tissue loss, though the processes underlying the variations in diffusivity are largely unclear, and II.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: alterations in the brain's microstructure may potentially provide new clues for a separation of normal aging from pathology.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: However, unless prospective studies are conducted, the issue remains open whether mean diffusivity is better as early predictor of AD than volume.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: It also would be premature to dismiss the value of anatomical MRI, because new developments in MRI have led to improvements in mapping the anatomy of the hippocampus, including differentiation of the subfields.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Hooshmand et al (2010) examined the relation between serum levels of homocysteine (tHcy) and holotranscobalamin (holoTC), the active fraction of vitamin B12, and risk of incident AD in a sample of Finnish community-dwelling elderly.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A dementia-free sample of 271 subjects aged 65 to 79 years derived from the Cardiovascular Risk Factors, Aging, and Dementia (CAIDE) study was followed-up for 7 years to detect incident AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The association between serum tHcy and holoTC with AD was analyzed with multiple logistic regression after adjusting for several potential confounders, including common vascular risk factors.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The odds ratios (ORs) (95 % confidence interval [CI]) for AD were 1.16 (1.04 to 1.31) per increase of 1 μmol/L of tHcy at baseline and 0.980 (0.965 to 0.995) for each increase of 1 pmol/L baseline holoTC.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Adjustment for several potential confounders including age, sex, education, APOE ε4 allele, body mass index, Mini-Mental State Examination, smoking, stroke, and blood pressure did not alter the associations: ORs (95 % CI) for AD became 1.19 (1.01 to 1.39) for tHcy and 0.977 (0.958 to 0.997) for holoTC.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Adjusting for holoTC attenuated the tHcy-AD link (OR changed from 1.16 to 1.10, 95 % CI: 0.96 to 1.25).
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The holoTC-AD relationship was less influenced by controlling for tHcy (OR changed from 0.980 to 0.984, 95 % CI: 0.968 to 1.000).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Addition of folate did not change any of the results.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that both tHcy and holoTC may be involved in the development of AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The tHcy- AD link may be partly explained by serum holoTC.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors stated that the role of holoTC in AD should be further investigated; further studies on the role of sensitive markers of B12 status in identifying individuals who are at increased risk of AD are needed.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In an editorial that accompanied the afore-mentioned study, Seshadri (2010) stated that observational studies and larger clinical trials are indicated, targeting older persons with MCI, simultaneously assessing holoTC (and B12) and plasma tHcy (and folate, methylmalonic acid).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Careful examination of the evidence is needed to ascertain who is the perpetrator in the complex pathology of AD and other dementias.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Perneczky et al (2011) examined if soluble amyloid precursor proteins (sAPP) in CSF improve the identification of patients with incipient AD in a group of patients with MCI.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A cohort study with follow-up assessments of 58 patients with MCI with baseline CSF sampling was conducted: 21 patients had progressed to probable AD (MCI-AD), 27 still had MCI, 8 had reverted to normal (MCI-NAD), and 2 patients with fronto-temporal dementia (FTD) were excluded.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Sixteen additional patients with FTD were included to explore the specificity of the CSF markers.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Cerebro-spinal fluid concentrations of sAPPα, sAPPβ, tau, and Aβ(1-42) were measured with sensitive and specific ELISAs.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Associations between diagnostic status, CSF protein concentrations, and other patient characteristics were explored using multiple logistic regression analyses with stepwise variable selection.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The optimal sensitivity and specificity of the best models were derived from receiver operating characteristic curves.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: The MCI-AD group had significantly higher sAPPβ concentrations than the MCI-NAD and the FTD groups.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: A combination of sAPPβ, tau, and age differentiated the MCI-AD and the MCI-NAD groups with a sensitivity of 80.0 % and a specificity of 81.0 %.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The best model for the differentiation of the MCI-AD and the FTD groups included sAPPβ and tau, and showed a sensitivity of 95.2 % and a specificity of 81.2 %.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Aβ(1-42) and sAPPα did not significantly contribute to the models.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that sAPPβ may be clinically useful, and superior to Aβ(1-42), in the early and differential diagnosis of incipient AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Limitations of this study included patient recruitment at a specialized memory clinic, which may restrict the generalization of the results to the general population with incipient AD and the lack of pathologic confirmation of AD and FTD.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Also, the recruitment of a modest number of patients and the relatively short follow-up period may have under-estimated the predictive value of sAPPβ.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors stated that replications of these findings in larger multi-center studies are needed.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: Further studies are needed to examine the clinical value of sAPPβ for the differentiation of AD from healthy aging and from other neurodegenerative disorders, and to investigate its use as a marker for anti-amyloid treatment response.
sentences.label: NON_ARGUMENTATIVE

sentences.text: In a case-cohort study, Schrijvers et al (2011) evaluated the potential of plasma clusterin as a biomarker of the presence, severity, and risk of AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Plasma levels of clusterin were measured at baseline (1997 to 1999) in 60 individuals with prevalent AD, a random subcohort of 926 participants, and an additional 156 participants diagnosed with AD during follow-up until January 1, 2007 (mean [SD], 7.2 [2.3] years).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Main outcome measures included prevalent AD, severity of AD measured by the Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE) score, and the risk of developing AD during follow-up.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The likelihood of prevalent AD increased with increasing plasma levels of clusterin (OR per SD increase of plasma clusterin level, 1.63; 95 % CI: 1.21 to 2.20; adjusted for age, sex, education level, apolipoprotein E status, diabetes, smoking, coronary heart disease, and hypertension).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Among patients with AD, higher clusterin levels were associated with more severe disease (adjusted difference in MMSE score per SD increase in clusterin levels, -1.36; 95 % CI: -2.70 to -0.02; p = 0.047).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Plasma clusterin levels were not related to the risk of incident AD during total follow- up (adjusted hazard ratio [HR], 1.00; 95 % CI: 0.85 to 1.17; p for trend = 0.77) or within 3 years of baseline (adjusted HR, 1.09; 95 % CI: 0.84 to1.42; p for trend = 0.65).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that plasma clusterin levels were significantly associated with baseline prevalence and severity of AD, but not with incidence of AD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: They stated that increased clusterin levels do not precede development of AD and thus are not a potential early marker of subclinical disease.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Tan and colleagues (2012) stated that higher dietary intake and circulating levels of docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) and eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) have been related to a reduced risk for dementia, but the pathways underlying this association remain unclear.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These investigators examined the cross-sectional relation of red blood cell (RBC) fatty acid levels to subclinical imaging and cognitive markers of dementia risk in a middle- aged to elderly community-based cohort.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: They related RBC DHA and EPA levels in dementia-free Framingham Study participants (n = 1,575; 854 women, age 67 +/- 9 years) to performance on
sentences.label: PREMISE
